payload.title: Monthly SRE review with Marcus
payload.content: Monthly review: SLA tracking 99.99% (one 4-min TLS incident). Two post-mortems published this period (P-7142, P-7218). Three runbook updates (v3.7 → v3.10). Marcus approved the cache-stampede preemptive work.

Tuned the disk-growth-rate alert added post-P-7218: changed from 7-day rate to a 24-hour delta with hourly evaluation. Catches WAL spikes faster while avoiding the false-positive from baseline weekly growth.

New section: when to use bluegreen vs rolling. Rolling = default (auth changes, schema-additive deploys). Bluegreen = high-risk (anything touching the LLM-compile pipeline or PgBouncer pool config). Tied to PR #334 landing.

PR #334: feat(deploy): add `fly deploy --strategy bluegreen` option in the runbook for zero-touch deploys during business hours. Riya: 'Optional — keep rolling as default; bluegreen for high-risk migrations only.'

Weekly rotation handoff 2026-05-04 09:00 UTC: primary US Riya → secondary EU Theo. No active incidents. One pending: monitor the lhr scaling behavior post-2026-05-08 deploy. Theo to verify p95 staying under 300ms in lhr through the week.

Datadog dashboard 'nimbus-api SLO' updated 2026-05-04 to reduce anomaly-detection sensitivity from 'medium' to 'low' on the request-rate widget — was firing on legitimate weekend traffic dips. p95/p99 widgets unchanged.
PR #327 merged 2026-05-09: feat(startup): pre-warm context cache before /readyz returns 200. Reduces cold-start tail latency from 1.2s to 280ms. Riya approved.

New runbook section added 2026-05-09 after a near-miss on 2026-05-07 where a cold-start in lhr coincided with a campaign-driven traffic spike. Mitigation: pre-warmed cache via a startup task that issues 5 representative /v1/context queries before health-check returns ready.
Scaled nimbus-api lhr from 1→2 machines on 2026-05-08 14:30 UTC. Verified: p95 in lhr dropped from 410ms (under load) to 290ms within an hour. iad unchanged at 220ms.

payload.title: Capacity planning: scale lhr 1→2 machines
payload.content: EU traffic up 40% MoM since BetaTech onboarding. Riya proposed scaling lhr from 1 to 2 nimbus-api machines pre-emptively. Approved by Marcus (Director of Infrastructure). Deploy scheduled for 2026-05-08 (Thursday, within the deploy window).

1-page postmortem (sev-4 lighter format). Action items: (1) add a 7-day disk-growth-rate monitor (done), (2) make the WAL retention 14 days instead of 30 to reduce baseline (deferred — needs compliance sign-off for the 30-day audit window).

Page 2026-04-30 11:08 UTC: disk >85% on nimbus-pg primary for 10m. Riya checked: WAL growth from a long-running ETL caused the spike. Mitigation: vacuum + WAL archive flush. Volume increased from 200GB to 250GB the next day to add headroom.
DATABASE_URL credentials rotated 2026-05-01 alongside the API key (same 90-day cadence, batched). Replica connection string updated last to avoid a stale pool window.

Riya rotated STATEWAVE_API_KEY on 2026-05-01 — the 90-day cadence triggered. Stage → verify staging /readyz → prod deploy in three steps. Old key revoked at 2026-05-01 18:00 UTC after all consumers confirmed adoption (4 consumers: nimbus-web, nimbus-admin, nimbus-billing, nimbus-eval).
Deploy 2026-04-23 14:12 UTC by Riya: fly deploy --remote-only. Health check passed; p95 returned to 220ms within 90s. Rolling strategy: 2/2 machines updated lease-by-lease. No downtime.

Updated 2026-05-05 after the DR drill. New step: confirm PgBouncer pool stats are quiescent before flipping the replication target (added because the drill showed a 90s spike of stale pooled connections post-promotion). Drill duration updated to 7 minutes (target <10).

payload.title: Quarterly DR drill
payload.content: Quarterly DR drill: Postgres replica promotion + region failover lhr → iad. Riya led. Total time 7 minutes (target <10). Drill notes added to runbook v3.8.

PR #312 merged 2026-04-22: chore(fly): cap llm_in_flight to 4. Riya approved. Deployment used the standard rolling strategy from the deploy runbook (no Friday afternoon, not in 02:00-04:00 UTC Wed maintenance).

New Datadog monitor created 2026-04-22: pages secondary on-call if upstream LLM 503 rate exceeds 5% for 3 minutes. Threshold tuned to fire on real provider degradation but not on single-request transient errors.

5-page post-mortem published 2026-04-21. Action items: (1) cap LLM in-flight requests at 4 by default in fly.toml (done), (2) add a Datadog monitor for upstream-503-rate (done, deployed 2026-04-22), (3) document the LLM-503 amplification pattern in the runbook (done, runbook v3.7).

Page 2026-04-19 03:42 UTC: error rate >2% for 5m on nimbus-api in iad. Riya (primary) took the page within 2 minutes. Root cause: a downstream LLM provider rate-limited 503ed; our backoff retry amplified. Mitigation: tightened in-flight cap from 8 to 4 concurrent compile requests (matches the v0.6 default).

SRE on-call lead Riya Patel (PT, primary US rotation) is the operator for nimbus-api as of 2026-04-15. Riya took over from the previous lead after the TLS-incident post-mortem; reports to Director of Infrastructure Marcus Chen.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Connection-pool tuning summary post-March: app-side pool=50 per machine, PgBouncer transaction-mode pool=100, statement_timeout=30s. Sustained 1200 RPS without exhaustion in load tests. Re-tune if RPS sustains >1500 for >30 minutes.

Scale-up checklist: (1) confirm Datadog p95 latency trend is sustained, not spiky; (2) check PgBouncer pool saturation in #ops dashboard; (3) `fly scale count <n> --region iad` — keep iad:lhr at 3:1 ratio; (4) verify with /readyz across all machines after rollout.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: SLA target: 99.95% monthly uptime for nimbus-api. Calculation excludes scheduled maintenance windows. Current month: 99.99% (one 4-minute incident on 2026-04-15, not customer-impacting).

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Migration policy: zero-downtime via alembic phased migrations. Three steps: (1) additive change deploys; (2) backfill runs as a job; (3) destructive change deploys in a follow-up. Never combine steps 1 and 3 in a single deploy. Burned by combining once in Q4 2025 — 8-minute write outage.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Standard alert thresholds (post-noisy-alert post-mortem): error rate >2% sustained 5m, p95 latency >300ms sustained 5m, p99 >800ms sustained 5m, disk >85% sustained 10m, replica lag >30s sustained 2m. Anything sub-5m is a graph, not a page.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Canary deploy framework launched for nimbus-api. New flow: `fly deploy --strategy canary` ships to one machine, soaks 5 minutes against a 1% traffic split, auto-rollbacks if error rate >0.5%. Reduced our last-mile rollback from 6 minutes (full redeploy) to 30 seconds (kill the canary machine).

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Drained iad-1 for OS upgrade (kernel CVE patch). Traffic shifted to iad-2/iad-3 + lhr. 22 minutes drain → upgrade → restore. No customer impact.

Fly-machines auto-restart on volume reattach: a routine OS upgrade on iad-3 triggered a volume detach/reattach that the nimbus-api process didn't survive cleanly. Lost ~40 in-flight requests. Mitigation: graceful-shutdown handler now waits up to 30s for in-flight requests to drain. Fly support confirmed this is expected on volume reattach.

Updated TLS rotation runbook: prod and staging now use separate Let's Encrypt issuer accounts so a staging-deploy storm can't rate-limit prod. Manual rotation (if auto fails): `fly certs add nimbus.example.com --app nimbus-api`, wait for verification, then `fly certs check`.

TLS auto-rotation failed on nimbus-api custom domain. Cause: Let's Encrypt rate-limited our domain after a misconfigured staging deployment fired 60 cert requests in an hour. Cleared by waiting out the 1-hour window + adding a staging-vs-prod issuer separation. No customer impact (cert hadn't expired yet — we caught the rotation failure 36h before).

Noisy-alerts post-mortem (2026-04-08): on-call paged 14 times in one weekend, 12 of which were transient blips. Root cause: alert thresholds were set per-host but nimbus-api auto-scales — a single slow request on a cold machine tripped the threshold. Fix: convert to fleet-wide percentiles, add 5-minute sustained-breach requirement before page.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Deploy-window policy: no Friday afternoon deploys (after 12:00 UTC). No deploys during weekly maintenance window 02:00-04:00 UTC Wednesdays. Emergency security patches exempt — but require dual sign-off in #ops-fire.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Capacity baseline post-PgBouncer: nimbus-api p95 latency 180ms, p99 450ms, sustained 1200 RPS at iad-primary. Anything above p95=300ms or p99=800ms warrants triage.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Observability stack decision: Datadog (logs + APM + dashboards) over Grafana Cloud. Reason: Datadog's anomaly detection on p95 latency caught the March outage 90 seconds before our static threshold would have. Re-evaluate in 2027 when the bill review happens.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Backup cadence locked: daily Postgres snapshots (retained 14 days) + WAL archive shipped continuously to S3 (retained 30 days). Restore tested monthly — last successful drill 2026-03-10, RTO 18 minutes for a 90GB instance.

Secrets rotation cadence: every 90 days. Process: (1) generate new value; (2) `fly secrets set KEY=value --stage --app nimbus-api` to stage; (3) verify staging env passes /readyz; (4) deploy. Rotated secrets: STATEWAVE_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, DATADOG_API_KEY. Set calendar reminder for next rotation 2026-06-12.

Postgres replica promotion runbook: (1) confirm replica lag <30s in Datadog; (2) `fly pg failover --app nimbus-pg --region lhr`; (3) update PGHOST in nimbus-api secrets to point at lhr-primary; (4) `fly deploy` nimbus-api to pick up new secret; (5) verify writes via /v1/healthz?writes=1 endpoint. Total time on last drill: 6 minutes.

March outage: connection-pool exhaustion under traffic spike (5x baseline at 14:00 UTC). nimbus-api ran out of Postgres connections; requests timed out for ~22 minutes. Mitigation: added PgBouncer (transaction pooling) in front of Postgres, raised app-side pool to 50, set statement_timeout=30s. Follow-up actions logged in incident #INC-2026-03-09.

Disk-pressure alert at 88% on iad-primary Postgres. Cause: WAL retention misconfigured (was 7 days, should be 2). Tuned to 2 days, freed 140GB. Threshold was also too low — bumped from 75% to 85% to reduce noise.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: On-call rotation: primary US (PT, weekdays), secondary EU (CET, weekdays + weekend coverage). Rotation handoff every Monday 09:00 UTC. Escalation path: PagerDuty → Slack #ops-fire → page CTO if a P0 incident persists past 30 minutes.

payload.author: sre-lead
payload.content: Region standardisation: nimbus-api runs in fly regions iad (primary) and lhr (secondary failover). No new regions without SRE-lead sign-off — adding regions has a non-trivial Postgres replica cost we haven't budgeted for.

To roll back the nimbus-api deploy, run `fly deploy --image-label <previous>` from the nimbus repo. Verify with /healthz before declaring green. Always check Datadog for p95 latency returning to <250ms before paging the original deployer.
